paragraph: Single-family homes in Fairview are moving at a median of $615,000 with a 1.5 percent sale-to-list ratio and a median of 16.5 days on market, which tells you the market is balanced. Half the homes sell above asking, half at or below, and sellers aren't holding out for outsized premiums. Multi-family properties sit higher at a $832,500 median across 12 sales in the period, with a 2.3 percent ratio and 20.5 days on market. Two-thirds of multi-family homes sell above list, so there's a bit more competition there, though the category includes everything from duplexes to larger apartment buildings, which can skew the median. By contrast, condos and townhouses are buyer-friendly territory. The median is $537,500, the ratio dips to 0.98, and homes sit for a median of 34.5 days. Only one in eight sold above asking. If you're looking at single-family or multi-family, you'll need to price fairly and be ready to move. If condos or townhouses appeal to you, you have more room to negotiate and time to choose carefully.
